channel.title: Spaceflight Now
channel.description: Spaceflight news, launch coverage, and analysis

item.title: Falcon 9 launches Starlink batch from Cape Canaveral
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX successfully launched a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station this afternoon, carrying a batch of Starlink internet satellites into low Earth orbit. Liftoff occurred on schedule at the opening of a five-minute launch window at 1:00 p.m. EDT.

The first stage booster, designated B1071, completed its eighteenth flight and successfully landed on the autonomous drone ship Just Read the Instructions stationed in the Atlantic Ocean. The Starlink satellites were deployed approximately 65 minutes after liftoff into their target insertion orbit.
